label: Rectum
quick_take: Rectum follows colon in most places, but there are a handful of registries where the two sites diverge — pointing to different underlying risk factors (or different coding practices). The most interesting anomaly is the female sex-ratio skew in Africa and the Caribbean.

findings.title: Japan again shows the inverted-U
findings.text: Miyagi men 6.47 → 24.47 (vol X) → 22.49 (vol XII). More gentle than the colon curve — consistent with the idea that screening turns colon around faster than rectum.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Japan again shows the inverted-U

findings.title: India Trivandrum women: U-shape collapse then partial rebound
findings.text: 22.0 (vol VII) → 1.77 (vol VIII) → 4.09 (vol XII). An 81 % fall then 130 % rise. Almost certainly reflects a registry-coding change between vol VII and VIII; flag for verification.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — India Trivandrum women: U-shape collapse then partial rebound

findings.title: Female-heavy sex ratios in Africa/Caribbean are striking
findings.text: Brazil Recife vol III M:F = 0.35 (women 3× men); Nigeria Ibadan 0.34; France Martinique 0.41; Uganda Kyadondo 0.39. Global vol-III median ratio is 1.5:1 male. Either a real biological signal or an ascertainment artefact (women more likely to reach registered care) — but consistent across multiple registries. Many of these ratios are computed from 4–20 female cases; treat as indicative, not publishable on their own.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Female-heavy sex ratios in Africa/Caribbean are striking

findings.title: Alaska Native and NWT women are the highest global outliers
findings.text: Alaska women 165→242 cases, ASR 10.19→9.84 (flat); the 23.2 / 24.0 peaks refer to earlier volume IV figures for Native-only subsets. The headline level (3× world median) is real; the specific magnitudes depend on the subset and volume chosen.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Alaska Native and NWT women are the highest global outliers

findings.title: Israel Jews born in Israel women: inverted-U with a vol-VII spike of 21.7
findings.text: That vol is flagged in pop_warning for Israel — double-check before claiming a real peak.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Israel Jews born in Israel women: inverted-U with a vol-VII spike of 21.7

findings.text: India Trivandrum vol VII vs VIII: the 22→1.77 step is suspicious; probably a scope change between the two volumes.
Israel pop_warning for vol V applies to the whole Israel file; the Israel-Jews-born-in-Israel vol-VII spike needs checking against the registry note.
Martinique 1975-era numbers have small denominators for ethnic/sex stratification.
A "colon vs rectum" pair-plot across CI5 XI–XII to see which registries break the 2:1 colon-to-rectum rule, and why.
Explore the sub-Saharan / Caribbean female-heavy signal across multiple related sites (colon, rectum) — might indicate an entirely different aetiological contributor.
Alaska Native / NWT excess: is it right-side colon creeping into rectal codes, or a genuine rectal excess?
